answer: Argon2id with parameters: 64MB memory, 3 iterations, 4 parallelism
7 days expiration with rotate-on-use rotation policy
Currently running PostgreSQL 15.4, upgraded from 14.9
5000 requests/min burst, 2000 requests/min steady state
Argon2id chosen for side-channel resistance compared to bcrypt
Drizzle chosen for zero runtime dependencies and SQL-like TypeScript interface
Both services validate JWT via JWKS endpoint (/.well-known/jwks.json) through the Lambda authorizer, which injects claims into requests
ALB rate limiting (1000/min/IP) prevents DDoS attacks before requests reach application containers, reducing resource consumption
Gateway validates schema -> Lambda authorizer validates JWT via JWKS and injects claims -> Portfolio Service receives authenticated request with user role claims for authorization check
JWKS endpoint down causes all downstream auth to fail across all services. Grace period: LRU cache with 1000 entry limit and 5-minute TTL. Token issuance continues using private key stored locally
Real-time portfolio alerts depend on WebSocket real-time portfolio alerts feature, requires both notify-service and gateway WebSocket API. Status: Planned for Q3
Yes, fixed by using Unix timestamps instead of Date objects to avoid UTC+13/UTC+14 timezone expiry bugs
Use the webhook registration endpoint (POST /api/v1/notifications/webhooks/register) with exponential backoff retry policy: 5 attempts over 24 hours
100 maximum connections (5 instances × 20 connections per instance pool limit)

Celery memory issue is unbounded during large portfolio recalc with tax-lot processing. Connection pool constraint of 20/instance limits concurrent database operations but doesn't directly resolve memory issue
No, Fargate Spot cannot be used for Auth Service because interrupt risk makes it unsuitable for always-on services. Fargate Spot is only for batch workloads like portfolio-recalc and digest
The RS256 multi-service JWT validation doctrine prohibits in-memory session cache because Auth Service must avoid caching auth state that other services might not have, maintaining statelessness
30-day backup retention supports RPO (1 hour) and RTO (4 hours) by providing recovery point options, with replication to us-west-2 for disaster recovery
sslmode=verify-full with global-bundle.pem after RDS upgrade to ensure encrypted TLS connections with certificate verification
id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, created_at
MFA check occurs after Argon2id password verify. If MFA enabled, user must complete TOTP verification with QR URI from POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/setup before receiving JWT tokens
10 recovery codes are generated. Each code is single-use and permanently consumed upon use
Exponential backoff with 5 attempts over 24 hours

At rest: AES-256-GCM encryption per-tenant keys for PII. Passwords: Argon2id(64MB/3iter/4par). In transit: mTLS via Envoy between services, sslmode=verify-full with global-bundle.pem for database connections

Gateway validates schema -> Lambda authorizer validates JWT via JWKS injects claims -> Auth Service /handlePostLogin -> Argon2id password verify -> MFA check if enabled (TOTP or recovery code) -> Issue RS256 access token (15min) and refresh token (7day rotate-on-use) -> Return to client

Naming: handleVerb{Resource}. Errors: RFC7807 Problem Details with traceId. Testing: real PostgreSQL via testcontainers. Logging: Pino structured JSON with AsyncLocalStorage request ID. Auth: JWT RS256 validation via JWKS. Rate limiting at ALB layer. Code review before merge

1500 req/min hits: ALB rate limit (1000/min/IP) drops excess requests before reaching service -> remaining requests pass through -> VPCLink to Auth Service on port 3847 -> Lambda authorizer validates JWT -> overflow queues or drops depending on service capacity

JWT signing key dependencies: Auth Service (signs) -> Lambda authorizer (validates via JWKS) -> Gateway (enforces) -> Portfolio Service (validates) -> Notification Service (validates) -> All downstream services validating tokens

GitHub Actions: lint -> typecheck -> test -> build -> push to ECR -> ECS deployment. Blue-green ALB target switch for zero-downtime. Claude Code review required before merge. Fargate Spot used only for batch jobs, not always-on services
